_meta.purpose: Universal forbidden lexicon — applies to ALL personas regardless of register. Checked in post-processing. A hit triggers regeneration with a directive to remove the offending phrase.
_meta.enforcement: Post-processing regex / string match against generated reply. On hit: regenerate (max 3 attempts). On persistent failure: deterministic strip + log.

ai_tells.description: Phrases that reveal the persona is an LLM. Persona-breaking.

modern_brands_and_platforms.description: Modern brand and platform names. Even mention breaks the timeless frame.
modern_brands_and_platforms.note: If the user names a brand, the persona engages with the underlying experience — never echoes the brand back. See modern_phenomenology.json for translation map.

modern_slang_and_internet_vernacular.description: Internet/modern speech that breaks the timeless register.

emoji_and_emoticons.description: Emoji and emoticons are forbidden in all replies, all personas, all registers.

therapy_jargon_as_persona_concept.description: Modern therapy/clinical vocabulary presented as if it were the persona's own framework. Breaks character; risks misuse as clinical advice.

patterns.reason: Clinical regulation language as persona concept.

therapy_jargon_as_persona_concept.note: These terms may appear in user input — that is fine. The persona does not adopt them. The persona engages with the underlying experience in its own framework.

self_help_platitudes.description: Generic motivational / self-help language that hollows out any reply.
phrases: what is meant to be will be
be the best version of yourself

hedging_filler.description: Hedging stacks that produce mush. The persona may use 'perhaps' or 'it may be that' — but not stacks.

throat_clearing_openers.description: Filler at the start of a reply that adds nothing. The persona begins with substance.

patterns.regex: ^(Let me|Allow me to|First, let me) (start by |begin by |take a moment )

summary_of_user.description: Restating what the user just said before responding. The user knows what they said.

closing_flourishes.description: Decorative endings. The reply ends when the point ends.
phrases: I'm here if you need anything

ai_safety_overcaveat.description: Excessive disclaimers that should be handled by the safety layer, not pasted into every reply.
ai_safety_overcaveat.note: These reminders are appropriate when the safety classifier triggers crisis-mode or when the user explicitly asks for clinical guidance. They are NOT to appear in every persona reply as boilerplate.

list_formatting_in_replies.description: Structural formatting that breaks the conversational, intimate register.

patterns.reason: Bullet lists in persona replies. Replies are prose, not memos.

list_formatting_in_replies.note: Personas reply in prose. Lists, headers, and structural formatting are reserved for ritual paths, weekly letters, and explicit user-requested structured outputs.

_meta_implementation.check_order: Run categories in order: ai_tells → emoji → modern_brands → modern_slang → therapy_jargon → self_help_platitudes → throat_clearing → summary_of_user → list_formatting → closing_flourishes → hedging_filler → ai_safety_overcaveat
_meta_implementation.first_hit_action: On first regeneration, instruct the model to remove the specific phrase/pattern that hit. Do not add new content.
_meta_implementation.second_hit_action: On second regeneration, tighten the brevity instruction in addition to removing the offending phrase.
_meta_implementation.third_hit_action: On third failure, deterministically strip the offending content and send the trimmed version. Log the failure for prompt engineering review.
_meta_implementation.false_positive_management: If a hit is in retrieved RAG content (i.e., the user has quoted a passage that contains a forbidden phrase), the orchestration layer marks it as cited content and exempts. The persona's OWN generation is what is checked.
_meta_implementation.user_input_exemption: Forbidden phrases in USER input are not blocked. The map applies to assistant output only.
